name: Google Dialogflow CX
pricing_notes: Text sessions $0.007/session; voice $0.06/min; Free: 1000 text sessions/mo; Enterprise discounts

auth.notes: Google Cloud service account credentials; Application Default Credentials (ADC) supported

performance.notes: Intent detection ~200ms; complex webhook fulfillment adds latency; real-time audio streaming available

agent_readiness.notes: Enterprise conversational AI platform. State-machine-based dialog management with Pages, Flows, and Intents. Webhook fulfillment for dynamic responses. Phone Gateway for telephony. Prebuilt agents for common use cases. REST API + Python/Node/Java SDKs. CCAI (Contact Center AI) integration. Better structured dialog control than LLM-only approaches.

gotchas: Complex state machine design requires significant upfront NLU design work
LLM-based NLU inferior to modern LLMs for complex understanding tasks
Google Cloud dependency — pricing and vendor lock-in concerns
Dialogflow ES (legacy) vs CX (current) confusion in documentation
best_when: Enterprise contact center automation with GCP commitment
Telephony bots requiring Google CCAI integration and phone gateway
Multi-language multilingual bots leveraging Google NLU
Teams needing visual dialog flow editor for non-technical stakeholders
avoid_when: Free-form conversational AI (modern LLMs more capable for open-ended dialog)
Small teams without GCP expertise (steep learning curve)
summary: Google Dialogflow CX is an enterprise conversational AI platform with state-machine-based dialog management (Pages, Flows, Intents). REST API + multi-language SDKs. Phone Gateway for telephony/CCAI integration. Prebuilt agent templates. Best for GCP-committed enterprises building contact center automation with visual flow editor and Google NLU. Modern LLMs surpass it for open-ended conversation; it excels at structured dialog control.
